title: 💻 Claude Code Best Practices: 12 Patterns Agentic Engineers Use
content:
Claude Code 技术干货

## Level Up Coding. ## CLAUDE CODE. # Claude Code Best Practices: 12 Patterns Agentic Engineers Use. ## Extracted from 69 Tips in GitHub’s #1 Trending Repo — with Input from Boris Cherny, the Engineer Who Built Claude Code at Anthropic. The third time I watched Claude delete the wrong branch, I stopped blaming the model. When something went wrong, I’d add another sentence to the prompt. * Only analyze, don’t modify files. * Don’t touch anything in `/cmd`. That word came from `shanraisshan/claude-code-best-practice` — a repo that hit GitHub Trending Day #1 in March 2026. Its subtitle: "from vibe coding to agentic engineering." The author had compiled 69 actionable tips across 11 categories, with input from Boris Cherny, the engineer who built Claude Code at Anthropic. ## Create an account to read the full story. If you’re new to Medium, create a new account to read this story on us. ## Published in Level Up Coding.

title: 💻 Claude Code Batch Processing #Linux - Qiita
content:
Claude Code 技术干货

Claude Code users often face the challenge of "wanting to execute multiple prompts sequentially" and "automating code generation with overnight batch processing." Many also need "project-specific prompt management" for their development workflows. Batch processing in Prompt Scheduler refers to **the functionality of pre-defining multiple prompts and automatically executing them in a specified order**. {"prompt": "Design and implement product management API", "tmux_session": "/tmp/tmux-1000/default", "sent": "false", "sent_timestamp": null, "default_wait": "20m"} {"prompt": "Implement payment system integration", "tmux_session": "/tmp/tmux-1000/default", "sent": "false", "sent_timestamp": null, "default_wait": "25m"} {"prompt": "Optimize product search functionality", "tmux_session": "/tmp/tmux-1000/default", "sent": "false", "sent_timestamp": null, "default_wait": "15m"} {"prompt": "Strengthen user authentication system", "tmux_session": "/tmp/tmux-1000/default", "sent": "false", "sent_timestamp": null, "default_wait": "20m"} {"prompt": "Propose admin panel UI improvements", "tmux_session": "/tmp/tmux-1000/default", "sent": "false", "sent_timestamp": null, "default_wait": "15m"}. # Overnight batch (aggressive execution ignoring limits) \ --prompt-file \ --mode \ --ignore-approaching-limit \ --hours # Phased execution during business hours \ --prompt-file \ --mode \ --stop-at. Prompt Scheduler v1.0.2's batch processing features dramatically improved development efficiency with Claude Code.

title: 💻 Claude Code Batch Processing Complete Guide: /batch Command and Headless Mode in Practice - SmartScope
content:
Claude Code 技术干货

# Claude Code Batch Processing Complete Guide: /batch Command and Headless Mode in Practice¶. The `/batch` command in Claude Code shines when you need to apply the same transformation across hundreds of files. | Aspect | /batch Command | Headless Mode (claude -p) |. `/batch` is a slash command executed within an interactive Claude Code session1. ## Headless Mode (claude -p): CI/CD and Script Integration¶. claude -p "Summarize this file" --output-format text. claude -p "Process this large dataset" --output-format stream-json`. claude -p "Analyze this code" --output-format json | jq -r '.result'. claude -p "Summarize this file" --output-format json | jq '.usage'`. session_id=$(claude -p "Start review" --output-format json \. claude -p "Continue the review" --resume "$session_id"`. claude -p "Review the code" \. - claude -p "Analyze maintainability issues" --output-format json. - claude -p "Check for performance bottlenecks" --output-format json. Claude Code's batch processing is built on two pillars: `/batch` and headless mode (`claude -p`). Claude Code v2.1.63 Release Notes — `/batch` and `/simplify` were introduced in this version.

title: 💻 Claude Code /simplify and /batch Commands Guide
content:
Claude Code 技术干货

# Claude Code /simplify and /batch: Bundled Commands That Ship Code Faster. Claude Code /simplify runs 3-agent code review on your changes. Boris Cherny, the Claude Code PM, put it well: these bundled commands "automate much of the work it used to take to shepherd a pull request to production." `/simplify` is the automated code review step between "it works" and "it's ready to merge.". Claude Code's `/batch` command orchestrates large-scale changes across your codebase in parallel. `/simplify` and `/batch` are bundled Claude Code commands -- they ship with every installation and work out of the box. `/simplify` and `/batch` shipped in Claude Code v2.1.63. `/simplify` and `/batch` signal where Claude Code is headed: bundled multi-agent workflows that handle common engineering patterns out of the box. If you want a development framework with pre-built agent orchestration, custom slash commands, and multi-agent patterns that work alongside these bundled commands, the ClaudeFast Code Kit ships with 18 specialized agents and a pipeline designed for exactly this kind of workflow.

title: 💻 Claude Code: Batch processing with Message Batches API | Claude Cookbook
content:
Claude Code 技术干货

# Batch processing with Message Batches API. Process large volumes of Claude requests asynchronously with 50% cost reduction using batches. Message Batches allow you to process large volumes of Messages requests asynchronously and cost-effectively. This cookbook demonstrates how to use the Message Batches API to handle bulk operations while reducing costs by 50%. Let's start with a simple example that demonstrates creating and monitoring a batch of message requests. `# Prepare a list of questions for batch processing. "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": question}],. print(f"Batch ID: {response.id}"). print(f"Status: {response.processing_status}"). Now let's monitor the batch processing status:. print(f" Processing: {batch_result.request_counts.processing}"). Once the batch is complete, we can retrieve and process the results:. batch = client.beta.messages.batches.retrieve(batch_id). print(f"Status: {batch.processing_status}"). for result in client.beta.messages.batches.results(batch_id):. print(f"Status: {result.result.type}"). print(f"Content: {result.result.message.content[0].text[:200]}..."). This example demonstrates more advanced usage, including error handling and processing different types of requests in a single batch including a simple message, a message with a system prompt, a multi-turn message, and a message with an image.
